title: Shuffling Data Between Epochs
concept: Shuffling data before each epoch ensures the model does not learn patterns based on the arbitrary order of rows, preventing biased weight updates.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why does a small batch size help escape local minima?

options.text: The noisy, bumpy gradients can bounce the model out of shallow plateaus.
It uses less total training data.

exercises.explain: Correct! Noisy gradients act like bumps that push the optimizer past shallow local traps.

cards.title: The stacked deck of cards
cards.body: If you try to learn how to play cards, and the deck is never shuffled, you'll start memorizing the sequence: 'a Red 3 is always followed by a Black Jack.' You aren't learning the rules of the game; you're just memorizing the order of the cards. To prevent this, we must shuffle our data before every epoch.

cards.title: Data Shuffling
cards.body: Shuffling is the process of randomizing the order of training rows at the start of each epoch. This ensures that each batch contains a different, random mix of examples, preventing the model from learning patterns based on row sequences.

cards.title: The vocabulary flashcards
cards.body: Think of studying flashcards. If you study them in the exact same order every day, your brain learns: 'card 4 is apple, card 5 is banana.' You start guessing the answer based on what card came before, rather than the word itself. Shuffling the deck forces you to evaluate each card independently.

cards.title: The grouped email trap
cards.body: A dataset has 100 spams followed by 900 normal emails. Without shuffling, Batch 1 contains only spam, pushing weights heavily toward 'spam'. Batches 2-10 contain only normal emails, warping the weights back. Shuffling guarantees each batch has a healthy mix, leading to smooth, balanced updates.

cards.title: Shuffling isn't for time series
cards.body: You might think shuffling is a universal rule for all ML. As we learned in Unit 48, for **time series data**, chronological order is the most important feature (the future depends on the past). Shuffling time series data breaks the sequence and destroys the model, so we only shuffle standard tabular or image datasets.

left.text:
Batch 1: All Spams.
Batch 2: All Normal.
Causes model to over-correct weights back and forth.

right.text:
Every batch has a random mix.
Stabilizes weight updates.
Model learns true features, not row order.

exercises.prompt: Why do we shuffle training data at the start of each epoch?

options.text: To ensure each batch has a random mix, preventing the model from learning patterns based on the arbitrary order of rows.
To automatically remove missing values from the dataset.
To speed up the GPU matrix multiplication calculations.
To change the model's loss function.

exercises.explain: Correct! Shuffling breaks row order bias, forcing the model to learn genuine features.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Shuffling data before training is highly recommended for time series forecasting models.
exercises.explain: False! Time series data relies on chronological order; shuffling breaks the temporal dependency and ruins the model.

exercises.sentence: If your training data is sorted (e.g. all fraud cases at the top), failing to ___ will cause unbalanced batch updates.
exercises.explain: Shuffling is essential to mix sorted data classes before dividing them into batches.

exercises.prompt: A dataset contains 100 images (50 cats, 50 dogs). Which batch creation setup is best?

options.text: Shuffle the 100 images randomly, then divide them into 5 batches of 20.
Keep all 50 cats first, producing 2.5 batches of only cats and 2.5 batches of only dogs.
Sort the images by file size before batching.

exercises.explain: Correct! Shuffling ensures every batch has a healthy mix of both categories, leading to stable updates.

exercises.prompt: You are training a classification model. The dataset is sorted by class label. In Epoch 1, the model gets stuck oscillating wildly. You add a shuffling step before each epoch, and the training curve stabilizes immediately. What caused this fix?

options.text: Shuffling mixed the classes, ensuring each batch gave a balanced gradient update, ending the violent weight swings caused by single-class batches.
Shuffling deleted the outliers that were causing the oscillations.
Shuffling converted the activation functions to ReLU.
Shuffling reduced the number of weights in the network.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Sorted batches cause the model to make extreme adjustments back and forth (e.g. all-spam updates vs. all-normal updates). Shuffling ensures each batch provides a balanced, stable correction signal. Next, let's learn how we track this progress over time using learning curves!
